details: Investigation begun. Symptom: UNPACK stuck in QK matmul_block. Snapshot committed.

Read compute kernel + matmul_block helper + eltwise_chain + matmul.h. Phase0 transform_in_place leaves unpacker in copy_tile(unpack_A) mode. matmul_block init_mode=Short reconfig=INPUT_AND_OUTPUT issues reconfig_data_format(in1,in0)+pack_reconfig(interm=q_buf)+mm_block_init_short. interm_buf==in0==q_buf. num_k_blocks=1 so interm never accessed for spill. in0_policy=WaitAndRetainOnLastBlock means Q never popped. UNPACK stuck in ckernel::matmul_block->llk_unpack_AB_matmul while MATH/PACK return.

H1: UNPACK stall is a unpack/math context-semaphore desync inside ckernel::matmul_block. _llk_unpack_AB_matmul_ posts UNPACK_SYNC per call and waits_for_next_context(2); _llk_math_matmul_ consumes. If UNPACK makes more posts than MATH gets, UNPACK stalls in wait_for_next_context after MATH exits. Likely caused by prior transform_in_place(unpack_A/copy mode) leaving unpack vs math MOP inconsistent that mm_block_init_short does not fully restore for the streamed operand. Need DEVICE_PRINT + waypoint confirmation.

DECISIVE: bisection prints show UNPACK blocks in cb_wait_front(cb_q_in, q_tiles=2) BEFORE the matmul. TR0 prints pre-matmul but never q-waited. MATH/PACK sail through (cb_wait_front near-noop on math/pack). Root cause is in cb_q_in front-count after Phase0 transform_in_place(in-place on cb_q_in): unpack does not see 2 tiles fronted. NOT a matmul-internal issue at all.

CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE: transform_in_place<cb_q_in> (in-place SFPU scale on the reader-fed Q CB) leaves cb_q_in with ZERO tiles visible to the next same-thread cb_wait_front. Proof: after transform, K (untouched) shows all tiles to UNPACK but Q shows 0 (cb_wait_front(cb_q_in,1) blocks). The in-place pop+push on a reader-fed input CB nets to zero available for the downstream matmul unpacker. The matmul UNPACK stall is downstream of this: it never gets Q. Disabling transform_in_place makes UNPACK see Q+K and pass the matmul wait region.

FIX: removed in-place pre-scale on cb_q_in; fold scale into cb_qk scores in-place after the QK matmul (Phase 1b). single_tile target PASSES, hang gone. Full suite: 20 passed / 12 failed. ALL passing cases are n_kv=1 (single_tile, multi_tile, multi_batch, gqa, mqa). ALL failures are n_kv>1 (non_square n_kv=2, long_seq n_kv=4, cross_attn n_kv=2) with PCC 0.2-0.93. Perfect n_kv axis correlation => the multi-block online-softmax recurrence (j>0 alpha-correction across KV blocks) has a SEPARATE pre-existing bug, unmasked now that the hang is gone. Scale-fold is per-block mathematically exact and is NOT the cause (n_kv=1 passes).

RESULT: hang fixed (HIGH confidence). Root cause: in-place pre-scale transform_in_place<cb_q_in> on reader-fed input CB starves the QK matmul unpacker. Fix: fold scale into cb_qk scores after the QK matmul. single_tile target PASSES in dev+prod mode. Cleaned all DEVICE_PRINT from kernel. Preserved debug test test_*_debug.py. Remaining: n_kv>1 cases fail PCC (separate online-softmax j>0 recurrence bug, out of scope for hang fix).
